title: Integrate GPT-5.6 structured requirement proposals

environment.os: Windows local; GitHub Actions Ubuntu and Windows

commands.name: Missing requirement proposal primitives
commands.command: pnpm exec vitest run --project unit tests/unit/requirement-proposals.test.ts --project property tests/property/requirement-proposals.property.test.ts
commands.expectedFailure: Both suites failed to import the not-yet-created requirement-proposals module and zero tests ran

commands.name: Undefined requirement proposal browser stories
commands.expectedFailure: Shared signed-session, software, and agreement setup passed while twelve AGR-02 workflow steps remained undefined

commands.name: Missing proposal repository and service boundary
commands.expectedFailure: All five scenarios reached the not-yet-implemented in-memory or PostgreSQL proposal repository constructor

commands.name: Missing signed proposal routes
commands.command: pnpm --filter @pactwire/core build; pnpm exec vitest run --project integration apps/web/tests/integration/requirement-proposal-routes.test.ts
commands.expectedFailure: The route suite could not import the not-yet-created agreement proposal endpoint

commands.name: Structured response, citation, pricing, and unsupported-output contracts
commands.command: pnpm exec vitest run --project unit tests/unit/requirement-proposals.test.ts --project property tests/property/requirement-proposals.property.test.ts

commands.name: Immutable proposal service and PostgreSQL persistence
Signed proposal HTTP boundary
Missing explicit web runtime adapter selector
commands.expectedFailure: All three cases reached the not-yet-exported runtime adapter selector

commands.name: Fail-closed deterministic and live web runtime selection
Missing encrypted-secret live workflow
commands.command: pnpm exec vitest run --project unit tests/unit/live-openai-workflow.test.ts
commands.expectedFailure: The contract could not read the not-yet-created live-openai workflow

commands.name: Live workflow could not run before merge
commands.command: pnpm exec vitest run --project unit tests/unit/live-openai-workflow.test.ts
commands.expectedFailure: The manual trigger alone could not execute a workflow absent from the default branch, so the contract required one isolated pre-merge trigger branch

commands.name: Encrypted-secret isolated live workflow contract
commands.command: pnpm exec vitest run --project unit tests/unit/live-openai-workflow.test.ts

commands.name: Ubuntu CI rejected ambiguous regex indentation
commands.command: GitHub Actions run 29720782899, Verify on ubuntu-latest: pnpm verify
commands.expectedFailure: ESLint no-regex-spaces rejected three literal YAML-indentation runs in the workflow contract before tests executed

commands.name: Explicit regex indentation and focused workflow verification
commands.command: pnpm exec eslint tests/unit/live-openai-workflow.test.ts --max-warnings=0; pnpm exec vitest run --project unit tests/unit/live-openai-workflow.test.ts --project integration tests/integration/evidence-harness.test.ts; pnpm evidence:check

commands.name: Successful proposal and refusal browser stories
Complete deterministic repository verification
Source-bound optimized-production browser capture
commands.command: PACTWIRE_CAPTURE_CURATED_EVIDENCE=1 PACTWIRE_EVIDENCE_TASK=AGR-02 PACTWIRE_BDD_PRODUCTION=1 PACTWIRE_BDD_TAGS=@AGR-02 pnpm test:bdd

commands.name: Clean-checkout verification on Ubuntu and Windows
Production dependency vulnerability audit
Tracked credential-pattern scan
commands.command: git grep -l -P with token-boundary secret-key and private-key patterns; no matches is success

commands.name: Required live GPT-5.6 Sol fictional PDF contract

scenarios.scenario: A structured proposal is tied to exact source text and stays non-executable
A model refusal creates a visible error and no proposal

assets.altText: Desktop requirement proposal panel showing one accepted non-executable fictional draft, an exact agreement quote with page and offsets, all structured fields, deterministic-adapter disclosure, token count, and estimated cost.
assets.caption: A deterministic fixture run proves the review interface and exact-citation gate without presenting the fixture as live GPT-5.6 evidence.

assets.altText: Narrow requirement proposal panel showing a visible model-refusal error, deterministic-adapter disclosure, zero stored proposals, and a manual source-review recovery message.
assets.caption: A refusal remains a failed, audited, non-executable run and leaves the stored agreement available for manual review.

proofs.description: Exact citation, strict Responses request, response classes, model identity, cost, and safe transport examples
Seeded invalid, incomplete, uncited, refused, unrelated, and missing-field non-materialization properties
Authorization, retry, cost aggregation, safe failure, immutable in-memory, and PostgreSQL proposal proof
Signed generation, listing, refusal, non-executable response, and reviewer authorization HTTP proof
Explicit live adapter selection, deterministic default, required server-side key, and unknown-mode rejection proof
Encrypted-secret, isolated-trigger, least-privilege, and sanitized-artifact workflow contract
Required opt-in real GPT-5.6 Sol fictional PDF structured-output and exact-citation contract
Exact structured proposal and visible safe-refusal workflows
Strict model boundary, exact locator, Responses transport, cost metadata, repositories, retry policy, and service
Immutable model-run ledger and successful-run-only requirement proposal guard
Direct-language proposal, authority-boundary, exact-source, cost, and safe-failure interface
Opt-in encrypted-secret live contract restricted to the dedicated pre-merge trigger branch or later manual dispatch
In-progress red/green record, OpenAI contract choices, and explicit live gate

knownLimitations: OPENAI_API_KEY is absent locally and no encrypted repository secret exists, so the required live GPT-5.6 Sol contract has not run and AGR-02 remains IN_PROGRESS.
The browser proof uses the explicitly labelled deterministic fixture adapter; it is not evidence of GPT-5.6 proposal quality.

Displayed cost is an estimate from reported tokens and the dated standard-tier price snapshot; the provider invoice remains authoritative and cache-write premiums are not separately identifiable from the response usage fields.
